title: Exodus: Red Sea Salvation
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of exodus: red sea salvation and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study exodus: red sea salvation and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What happened to the Egyptian army that pursued Israel at the Red Sea? Answer: the waters returned and drowned the entire army when Israel had passed safely through.
segments.correct_answer: the waters returned and drowned the Egyptian army
acceptable_variants: they were swallowed by the sea when the waters came back
the sea closed on pharaoh's chariots and horses
the Egyptian army was destroyed in the sea

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The victory hymn in Exodus 15 celebrating Israel's deliverance at the sea; one of the oldest poems in Scripture and a model for praise after divine rescue
The Hebrew name for the body of water Israel crossed; often translated Red Sea; the site of Israel's decisive deliverance from Egypt
